title: Magic Kingdom Rides and Attractions in 2026
description: Today is ALL about the rides, shows, and characters inside Disney World’s Magic Kingdom! contentSnippet: Today is all about the rides, the shows, and the characters in Disney World's Magic Kingdom. So, join us for a trip around this fan favorite park to ride, see, and do it all. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. This is Magic Kingdom, the Disney World park with the most rides, the most animatronicled stage productions, and the most attractions for all ages. So, join us as we explore each Magic Kingdom land to check out their shows, characters, and rides, broken up between those with and without height requirements, and make sure to keep track of all your mustdos as we go along. If you want help keeping all those must dos organized as you jot them down, go ahead and scan the QR code you see on the screen right now or click on the link in the description for Disneyfoodblog.co...
transcript: Today is all about the rides, the shows, and the characters in Disney World's Magic Kingdom. So, join us for a trip around this fan favorite park to ride, see, and do it all. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. This is Magic Kingdom, the Disney World park with the most rides, the most animatronicled stage productions, and the most attractions for all ages. So, join us as we explore each Magic Kingdom land to check out their shows, characters, and rides, broken up between those with and without height requirements, and make sure to keep track of all your mustdos as we go along. If you want help keeping all those must dos organized as you jot them down, go ahead and scan the QR code you see on the screen right now or click on the link in the description for Disneyfoodblog.com/discoplans after this and we can send you our free Disney World planning worksheets that should do wonders for keeping your park priorities in check. We're going to start, of course, in Main Street USA. Main Street USA might not have any thrill rides that you'll need to put on the top of your priority list, but that doesn't mean there aren't any attractions in this turn of the century front of parkland. Now, there aren't rides per se in Main Street USA, but there are transportation methods you can hop aboard. In the mornings or early afternoons, you can make your way towards Cinderella Castle in a horsedrawn trolley, double-deck or omnibus. That one's my favorite, the Jity, or the Main Street Fire Engine number 71. Because of course, 1971 is when Magic Kingdom opened. Or you can take a vehicle back towards Town Square from the Cinderella Castle Hub. Here's a pro tip for you. If you want to visit with the Main Street USA horses for a little bit longer during your vacation, take a boat or bus over to Fort Wilderness and pay them a visit at the Tric Circle D Ranch, which is absolutely free to go check out. And then there's the Walt Disney World Railroad, which has a station right above Magic Kingdom's park entrance. Normally, there are three stations the steam train will take you to around the park, a Fantasy Land station, and a Frontier Land station in addition to Main Street, but since Frontier Land is in major construction mode right now, that particular station is closed until further notice. Now, instead of making a full loop around the park, the train operates in shuttle mode right now between Main Street USA and Fantasy Land. The live show offerings and streets sphere in Main Street, USA are really where this land shines. Usually, you can expect a couple different stage shows to happen in front of Cinderella Castle each day. Let the Magic Begin is the park opening show. Just a little something to get you hyped for the big day ahead of you. And Mickey's Magical Friendship Fair is a musical performance led by Mickey, Minnie, and other classic Fab Five members who are joined by even more beloved characters like friends
notes: General MK rides walkthrough video — no new information beyond what's already in our data

transcript: You did not come all this way to Disney World to spend all this money just to have bad food. That's unacceptable. And we're putting a stop to it right now. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. So just because you're paying for food in the Disney World bubble doesn't mean you're automatically guaranteed to get good food. There are hundreds of places to dine and snack and drink all around Disney World property, and not all of them are going to hit it out of the ballpark. So, today, I'm going to steer you away from any foods that you might regret purchasing, and show you how to track down the stuff that'll make you nostalgic for another bite long after your trip is over. That's why you rely on us, right? We're in the parks every single day eating this food all the time, and we're going to make sure you know the right stuff to spend your money on. First up, I think is something nobody really talks about, but is very, very important cuz you know what? It's expensive. Disney World's got some good buffets. And they have some really good buffets like Bulma at Animal Kingdom Lodge, 1900 Park Fair at Grand Fidian, Beer Garden at Epcot, Tusker House in Animal Kingdom. But even a really good buffet can be diminished just by timing your visit badly. When a buffet is getting ready to close, so we're talking the last hour or less of the night, cast members are going to stop restocking those buffet lines with super fresh stuff since they're wrapping up with their final tables of the night and they're getting ready to start cleaning up the kitchen in the back. So, you might not have as fresh of the food to pick out on your buffet line in that last hour or so of operations. and we're just giving you this information not because we think that Disney is trying to trick you or anything, but because we've gone to lots of buffets late at night, like right before they close, and this is the experience we've had. So, try to hit a buffet at least 2 hours before it closes in order to guarantee you get the freshest stuff. Now, here's the other side of that point. because food demand isn't as high, then the demand for the meet and greet characters isn't as high late at night either. If you're at a buffet with character dining, that is. So, if character interactions are more of what you're after, then a late night dining reservation for a buffet could be a whole lot less busy and should give you more interaction with the characters, sometimes even all of the characters at once because there's no one else for them to hang out with. So, they might just all come over to your table and mass. That's happened to me before and it's pretty fun. Now, piggybacking off of that last point, I understand that sometimes we choose Disney World restaurants more for the overall atmosphere or experience or storytelling instead of the food quality. transcript: You're being brainwashed by the Disney parks. How? Well, we're exposing the truth today on DFB Guide. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Vlog. Ready to learn about a whole lot of stuff Disney' rather you not know anything about? because I'm going to teach you a thing or two about Disney's masterful art of manipulation, which can be useful to you while you're in the parks, but it's always going to be extremely useful to them. Y'all remember Disney Genie? If you do, then you probably remember it best as Disney Genie Plus, the first My Disney Experience add-on that introduced us to the premium skip the ride line service, aka paid fastpass or lightning lanes. Disney Genie Plus no longer exists. It's been replaced by multiass and single pass. Premiere pass since then, but the free version of Disney Genie is actually still around. No, seriously. Go to the home screen of the My Disney Experience app and scroll down just a tad and you'll see towards the top of the page an option to summon Disney Genie services, which is a complimentary tool to keep tabs on your daily itinerary. Dot dot dot. This function can actually be pretty cool. It helps craft an itinerary for you based on your personal preferences and reservations. And if your plans happen to change on the day of your visit or as the day changes regarding what's going on in the parks, Genie can adjust your schedule while you're still on the go. And if you allow the app to send you push notifications to your phone, then your Genie will provide you with realtime tips and updates and recommendations to help you pivot when you need to. Sounds great, right? So, here's where the whole sneaky controlling part comes into play. According to the Wall Street Journal, each of the Disney parks has an operation center with a heat map, which tells operators where Lightning Lane users are in the parks using GPS technology. Park operators then use this information to direct traffic by alerting guests via push notifications about the shortest lines and food and merchandise promotions to spread crowds out around the park. For example, let's say you're in Magic Kingdom and everyone is just hovering around Frontier Land because the Festival of Fantasy Parade just wrapped up and now everybody's heading into the lines for Tiana's Bayou Adventure and Big Thunder Mountain Railroad. Disney Genie and the My Disney Experience app in general can suggest that you check out an attraction or restaurant in Tomorrowland for fewer crowds and immediate service, which is on the far opposite end of where Frontier Land is. This could help alleviate bottlenecked pathways and keep one or two attractions from being completely overwhelmed with guests. Now, this actually does happen. There's plenty of times that we've been in the parks and one area of the park is completely overwhelmingly crowded and packed and another area of the park is like a ghost town. No
notes: Opinion/analysis video — how Disney manages crowds

Now, before we get into the things that will be changing around Disney's hotels in 2026, let's quickly talk about all the things that have already changed in the past year that will still impact your upcoming stay, just in case you haven't been there over the past year. Right. First off, in 2025, hotel only reservations, yep, those not tied to a vacation package with theme park tickets, we also call them room only reservations, switched their cancellation policy rules on us. Before, you could still get a full refund when canceling your room up to 5 days before check-in. But now it must be cancelled eight days before your scheduled check-in to receive a full refund. If you cancel between zero and seven days, you will now lose the cost of a one night stay, which also serves as a deposit. Last year was when Disney's Value Resort started working with domestic Southwest Airlines flights in Orlando International Airport to provide a skip baggage claim perk, meaning Southwest passengers can have their bags delivered directly to their value resort and back to the airport again at the end of their vacation, minus a whole lot of extra hassle. If you want to learn more about how this new bag perk works, then be sure to check out our post all about it on the DFB website. I'll link that post for you down in the description below. Porter's French Quarter experienced quite a fixerupper last year across its lobby and rooms. Now you'll see more vibrant rug decorations, a brighter lobby, and rooms that embrace that Louisiana charm with new pops of color and Princess and the Frog influences. 2025 was a huge year for Pop Century. It received room updates, a whole food court makeover, and its lobby got a whole paint job, too, which took away the old school shadow boxes with relics from the past. And that makes our hearts hurt. transcript: When you're in Disney World, there is no escape from the continuous eyes of the mouse. But maybe it's better that way. Here's a behind-the-scenes look into how Disney World security is able to keep tabs on you all vacation long and why that's crucial information for your upcoming trip. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. Thousands upon thousands of guests enter the Disney World parks every single day. And yet, Disney still has their sights set on you. Yep, you. But I'm not here to just tell you that. I'm here to prove it. While also assuring you that you're going to be 110% grateful for the security spotlight when all is said and done. Outside of every single Disney World park, before you reach the front gate, Tap Styles, you'll encounter a security checkpoint. This checkpoint is made up of a dozen odd security cast members as well as a few walkthrough security detectors like you'd find at the airport. But thankfully, you don't have to take off your shoes for this one. Instead, you will probably benefit from taking things out of your park bag that might set security detectors off, like any large electronics like portable chargers and tablets, metal items including mint tins, cases for your sunglasses or glasses, umbrellas, or really just anything that you're questioning if it'll trigger security or not. I mean, if you got the hands to hold those items and walk them through the security checkpoint out of your bag, you might as well. So, what happens then if you accidentally leave one of these electronics or metal items in your bag, and it does trigger security? Well, you're not in trouble or anything. Owning an umbrella is not a federal offense yet, but you will be directed to enter a second line where you'll go through a more thorough bag check before you're clear to go. During this more thorough bag check, if a security member finds something in your bag you're not allowed to have, like glass containers, or outside alcohol, drug paraphernalia, selfie sticks, or something else that's banned from the parks, you're not going to get wheeled off to the brrig or anything, unless it's really bad, of course. But you will be asked to either dispose of the banned item or backtrack to your vehicle or hotel room to put it away before you're allowed to start your day. And that's a major time suck. So, just don't bring banned items. Maybe we have a whole video about what's banned, so you can just watch that and make sure you don't have that stuff. Now, if you're bringing a stroller or a wheelchair with you to the parks that day, those will not go through the same security detectors. Instead, you'll have to take them through a different line, which is usually just directly to the left or right of the regular security checkpoint, and you'll have it more thoroughly inspected by a security cast member. Kids in the strollers will still be requir
notes: General security overview — no new policy changes, just educational content about bag checks and surveillance.

contentSnippet: Do you want to go on a Disney World vacation, but you're already exhausted thinking about all the lines and the crowds and the lack of personal space? We're here to show you how to survive Disney World, even when it's packed. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. There are three things that I really have to brace myself for every time I go to Disney World. Price hikes, heat exhaustion, and crowds. We're talking so many people everywhere. Even during their slow seasons, Disney World still has thousands upon thousands of guests swarming their parks each day. transcript: Do you want to go on a Disney World vacation, but you're already exhausted thinking about all the lines and the crowds and the lack of personal space? We're here to show you how to survive Disney World, even when it's packed. Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. There are three things that I really have to brace myself for every time I go to Disney World. Price hikes, heat exhaustion, and crowds. We're talking so many people everywhere. Even during their slow seasons, Disney World still has thousands upon thousands of guests swarming their parks each day. But if navigating those crowds is the sole reason why you haven't locked in your big Disney World vacation yet, let me show you how to juggle the parks, the hotels, and Disney Springs in ways that'll even make the busiest day more manageable and more fun. So, Epcot has two separate entrances. The more popular and more crowded entrance tends to be at the front of the park, which will put you right where Spaceship Earth is, and the less popular and therefore less crowded entrance is at the back of the park, Epcot's International Gateway, which is located right between the France pavilion and the UK pavilion. The World Showcase is always less crowded before everything officially opens up for the day since most pavilion offerings won't open till 11:00 a.m. However, the rides in all of those World Showcase Pavilions open right as early theme park entry starts. So, if you use the International Gateway, you'll be right there next to Remy's Ratatouille Adventure just as soon as you enter into Epcot, giving you a straight shot at being one of the first folks in line for it at the start of the day. Then you can go over to Lelay all and have a nice relaxing breakfast and then wander through the World Showcase a little bit before all the crowds make their way over there. Waiting in long lines for characters is not the most fun. Waiting no time for the characters with way fewer people surrounding you, super fun. Now, in the past, being able to see Disney characters pop up around the hotels was a nice hidden treat, but nothing was ever guaranteed. But lately, some of the Disney resorts have scheduled character appearances, so you don't have to gamble on whether or not you'll be visiting a resort when one of these free meet and greets is taking place. Normally, scheduled character appearances take place at Porteran's French Quarter and Wilderness Lodge. But during Cool Kids Summer, taking place from May 26th to September 8th, you'll also be able to find scheduled character meet and greets at additional hotels, including Art of Animation, Caribbean Beach, Pop Century, and Porter's Riverside. Okay, this one's weird, I know, but in our personal experience, and as you know, we've got reporters in the parks all day, every day, Disney resorts that have convention centers do tend to have fairly peaceful locations with little to no guests
notes: Crowd tips — EPCOT gateway strategy already in our data, Cool Kids Summer is May-Sep only (not Jan), no new actionable info.

Hey everybody, it's AJ for Disney Food Blog. We've got important updates from around Disney World that will affect your trip. But first, we're starting with our favorite kind of news to share, festival menus. Let's dive in. This is not a drill. We officially have menus for the Epcot International Flower and Garden Festival. Thank goodness because it starts like in a week and a half. Anyway, the 2026 Epcot Flower and Garden Festival runs from March 4th to June 1st. The festival's full of gorgeous flowers and topiaries, special entertainment, and lots of delicious food and drinks. We've been eagerly awaiting the full menus for this year's festival, and the time has officially arrived. Some fan favorites are returning like the trio of dips and chips, lemonchello sparkling cocktail with basil this time, avocado toast, ceviche, hibiscus lemonade cocktail, and chicken and waffles. But there are over 70 new items coming to the festival this year, including a brand new booth called Nectar. Here's a sneak peek at their dessert. The coconut panacotta is made with cherry gel and matcha crumb topped with a citrus cookie, plant-based mering, and flour confetti. Other new items or newish twists on old items include a black and fish slider, strawberry basil lemonade, cola miso ribs, and coconut chocolate fla. Cannot wait for opening day to try all the amazing food. Be sure you're following us on social media to see all of our opening day coverage. You know, we go to every single booth, we eat everything, and we get you the best of the fest immediately. We've got a progress update on Cinderella Castle herself. You can now see the new grave from the front of the castle. It's not super obvious just yet from the front, but if you're really looking for it, you will find it from the side. You can clearly see the split between the two colors. Depending on what's next in the repainting process, the castle might be entering its awkward phase from the front, but it's all part of the process. You ready for the nostalgic gray to be back, or are you already missing that pink? A new snack spot just opened in the Norway Pavilion in Epcot. It's located right at the front of the pavilion with a mossy green roof. Can't miss it. They're serving up the iconic school bread, which you can also grab at Cringa Bakery. It tasted absolutely delicio
